sources.name: TUC / University of Hertfordshire
sources.dataset: Living on the Gig Economy Survey

metadata.methodology: Gig worker estimates combine ONS Labour Force Survey data on self-employed workers in gig-associated occupations with academic survey estimates. Hourly pay figures for gig workers are net of work-related costs (vehicle, equipment, data) based on TUC/University of Hertfordshire survey methodology. Employee hourly pay uses ONS ASHE median hourly earnings for all employees.
knownIssues: There is no official ONS definition or consistent measurement of 'gig economy' workers; estimates vary substantially between surveys and methodologies
The 2021 Supreme Court Uber ruling changed the legal status of some gig workers but did not change ONS survey classification
Net pay calculations depend on cost assumptions that vary by sector and are based on self-reported survey data
